content.assessment: This proposal targets the capital-intensive entry competition (F-live-equity_and_social_mobility-03) directly, without touching the tracking age itself, so it addresses one sorting mechanism (SES-correlated access, F-live-equity_and_social_mobility-02) while leaving early tracking's structural inequality association untouched (F-live-equity_and_social_mobility-01). Because sorting is shown to relocate rather than disappear when partially addressed (F-live-equity_and_social_mobility-10), gains from coaching-resistant exam design are plausible but likely partial and vulnerable to adaptive elite response.
opportunities: Could be layered with geographic-access measures given documented rural-urban disparities in track proximity (F-live-equity_and_social_mobility-08)
Could reduce, though not eliminate, the family-capital investment race preceding formal testing (F-live-equity_and_social_mobility-03)
strengths: Directly targets the documented SES-correlated access advantage at the point of entry (F-live-equity_and_social_mobility-02)
Addresses disadvantaged-school non-participation, a concrete access barrier prior to formal testing (F-live-equity_and_social_mobility-03)
Reduces coachable/subjective components that privilege capital-rich families without requiring structural change
threats: Elite families are documented to adapt strategies to formal rule changes, likely eroding gains over time similar to the general pattern where selective-track advantage attenuates or relocates (F-live-equity_and_social_mobility-05, F-live-equity_and_social_mobility-10)

Since selection itself relocates rather than eliminates advantage-concentration (F-live-equity_and_social_mobility-04), a redesigned admissions process may simply shift who is advantaged within the same segregating structure
weaknesses: Leaves the age-10/12 selection point itself intact, so the core inequality-associated mechanism persists (F-live-equity_and_social_mobility-01)
Does not address segregation originating in primary-school choice, a documented independent sorting mechanism (F-live-equity_and_social_mobility-08)
Does not address Roma segregation, which is distinct from SES sorting and concentrated system-wide regardless of admission-rule design (F-live-equity_and_social_mobility-06)
